title: Regulatory Incident Response Time Trending

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.33 requires breach notification to the supervisory authority 'without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours'. Trending regulatory incident response times evidences that the organisation measures and is meeting that obligation over time.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.33 (Breach notification to supervisory authority) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.9.4: Regulatory Incident Response Time Trending.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.9.4' running on notable — filter with tag/category/rule_name for compliance/regulatory work; fields closed_time, _time, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.23 mandates phased incident reporting (24h early warning, 72h notification, 1-month final report). Trending MTTR and MTTF (mean time to first report) against these windows is the operational evidence of compliance.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.23 (Reporting obligations) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.9.4: Regulatory Incident Response Time Trending.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.9.4' running on notable — filter with tag/category/rule_name for compliance/regulatory work; fields closed_time, _time, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `` `notable` `` — filter with `tag`/`category`/`rule_name` for compliance/regulatory work; fields `closed_time`, `_time`
spl:
`notable` earliest=-730d (tag="compliance" OR category="*compliance*" OR rule_name="*regulatory*" OR like(rule_name,"%compliance%"))
| eval mttr_sec=if(isnotnull(closed_time) AND closed_time>_time, closed_time-_time, null())
| where isnotnull(mttr_sec)
| timechart span=90d avg(mttr_sec) as avg_mttr_sec perc95(mttr_sec) as p95_mttr_sec
| eval avg_mttr_h=avg_mttr_sec/3600
| trendline sma2(avg_mttr_sec) as mttr_trend
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(avg_mttr_sec) as prev_q_mttr
| eval vs_prev_q_pct=if(isnotnull(prev_q_mttr) AND prev_q_mttr>0,round(100*(avg_mttr_sec-prev_q_mttr)/prev_q_mttr,1),null())
| predict avg_mttr_sec as mttr_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=2 period=4
description: Mean time to resolve compliance-tagged incidents by quarter proves that regulatory and policy breaches are handled with discipline—supporting supervisory expectations and internal KPIs beyond generic IT MTTR.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR, NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: Mean time to resolve compliance-tagged incidents by quarter proves that regulatory and policy breaches are handled with discipline — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Define a consistent ES tag or naming convention for regulatory notables; (2) confirm `closed_time` population for closed incidents (`| fieldsummary closed_time`); (3) exclude false positives with a lookup of excluded `rule_name` values; (4) compare quarterly MTTR to IT-wide MTTR in a separate panel for context; (5) document scope (which jurisdictions or policies) in the dashboard subtitle.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` — filter with `tag`/`category`/`rule_name` for compliance/regulatory work; fields `closed_time`, `_time`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define a consistent ES tag or naming convention for regulatory notables; (2) confirm `closed_time` population for closed incidents (`| fieldsummary closed_time`); (3) exclude false positives with a lookup of excluded `rule_name` values; (4) compare quarterly MTTR to IT-wide MTTR in a separate panel for context; (5) document scope (which jurisdictions or policies) in the dashboard subtitle.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` earliest=-730d (tag="compliance" OR category="*compliance*" OR rule_name="*regulatory*" OR like(rule_name,"%compliance%"))
| eval mttr_sec=if(isnotnull(closed_time) AND closed_time>_time, closed_time-_time, null())
| where isnotnull(mttr_sec)
| timechart span=90d avg(mttr_sec) as avg_mttr_sec perc95(mttr_sec) as p95_mttr_sec
| eval avg_mttr_h=avg_mttr_sec/3600
| trendline sma2(avg_mttr_sec) as mttr_trend
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(avg_mttr_sec) as prev_q_mttr
| eval vs_prev_q_pct=if(isnotnull(prev_q_mttr) AND prev_q_mttr>0,round(100*(avg_mttr_sec-prev_q_mttr)/prev_q_mttr,1),null())
| predict avg_mttr_sec as mttr_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=2 period=4
```

Understanding this SPL

**Regulatory Incident Response Time Trending** — Mean time to resolve compliance-tagged incidents by quarter proves that regulatory and policy breaches are handled with discipline—supporting supervisory expectations and internal KPIs beyond generic IT MTTR.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` — filter with `tag`/`category`/`rule_name` for compliance/regulatory work; fields `closed_time`, `_time`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Invokes macro `notable` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **mttr_sec** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(mttr_sec)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=90d** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **avg_mttr_h** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

• `streamstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **vs_prev_q_pct** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: avg_mttr_h, mttr_sec, vs_prev_q_pct.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (`avg_mttr_h` or `avg_mttr_sec`, `mttr_trend`, `mttr_forecast`), column chart (`p95_mttr_sec` by quarter), single value (`vs_prev_q_pct`).
visualization: Line chart (`avg_mttr_h` or `avg_mttr_sec`, `mttr_trend`, `mttr_forecast`), column chart (`p95_mttr_sec` by quarter), single value (`vs_prev_q_pct`).
knownFalsePositives: Trends shift during new framework adoption, scope changes, or after major audit findings remediation.
